mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) over the prior three months signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO over 3-month windows do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX forward returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.5% indicates substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage-driven consumption and compresses corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.25% and a rising consumer price index generates negative real policy rates, which forces investors out of cash and Treasuries into risk assets, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL higher than prior month
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rising month-over-month fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a deep recession, month-over-month payroll employment growth signals a durable demand recovery that reduces tail-risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-shock recovery periods defined by UNRATE above 7%, positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes coincide with negative 60-day SPX forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% traps short-term yields near zero, eliminating risk-free income and mechanically increasing the relative present value of equities, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered weeks over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating retail sales growth exhausts pent-up demand and tightens input markets, leading to margin compression expectations that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods where UNRATE has fallen by more than 2 percentage points from its trailing 24-month high, month-over-month RSAFS increases coincide with positive 5-day SPX forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in industrial production and housing starts signals a broad-based rebound in both manufacturing and construction activity, reinforcing expectations of above-trend GDP growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in INDPRO and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day SPX forward returns in more than 50% of coincident observations over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a stalled residential construction sector that drags on aggregate demand and employment spillovers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 600 thousand units, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.
